{"query":"LVMH Hennessy new product category launch July 2026 wines and spirits division","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1961,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://drinks-intel.com/cross-category/moet-hennessy-starts-2026-on-front-foot-results-data/","title":"Moët Hennessy starts 2026 on front foot - results data - Global Drinks Intel","content":"Moët Hennessy starts 2026 on front foot - results data - Global Drinks Intel
Moët Hennessy has enjoyed its best quarter of sales since Q3 of 2022, starting 2026 with a mid-single-digit rise.
The group, which in January booked a fall in sales for 2025 of 5%, said late today (13 April) that its top line in the three months to the end of March was up by the same rate versus the corresponding quarter in 2025.
The 15-day celebrations commenced on 17 February compared to 29 January in 2025."},{"url":"https://www.lvmh.com/en/news-lvmh/hennessy-opens-a-new-chapter-with-its-first-ready-to-serve-cocktails","title":"Hennessy opens a new chapter with its first ready-... - LVMH","content":"More than 260 years after its founding, Hennessy is opening a new chapter in its history with the launch of Hennessy Very Special Cocktails, a collection of ready-to-serve cocktails crafted from Hennessy Very Special and the first Hennessy creation to combine cognac with other ingredients.
For Charles Delapalme, President and CEO of Hennessy, the move is also rooted in the Maison’s history.
With Hennessy Very Special Cocktails, the Maison continues to explore new ways of enjoying cognac, introducing it to new audiences and new occasions."},{"url":"https://www.thespiritsbusiness.com/2026/04/lvmh-starts-2026-with-spirits-uplift/","title":"LVMH starts 2026 with spirits uplift - The Spirits Business","content":"LVMH starts 2026 with spirits uplift
LVMH’s spirits revenue totalled €610 million (US$719.3m) for Q1, which was down from €629m (US$741.6m) year on year.
The company’s Champagne and wine organic revenue reached €663m (US$781.6m) – up by 5%.
The performance was led by 7% growth in Asia (excluding Japan), LVMH’s biggest market with 32% of total revenue, along with a 3% gain in the US – its second-largest region with 23% of sales."},{"url":"https://www.firstleaf.com/a/wine-directory/wineries-and-brands/lvmh-moet-hennessy","title":"LVMH Moët Hennessy: Luxury Wines, Spirits & 2026 Updates | Firstleaf Wine Guide","content":"LVMH Moët Hennessy is the world’s leading luxury wine and spirits powerhouse, representing the 'Wines & Spirits' business group of the LVMH empire.
Headquartered in Paris, the group balances centuries of heritage—such as the 1365 origins of Clos des Lambrays—with 21st-century innovation.
The portfolio also includes Glenmorangie and Ardbeg whiskies, Belvedere vodka, and the ultra-luxury Volcan de mi Tierra tequila.
The Arnault Vision — Since February 2025, the appointment of Alexandre Arnault as Deputy CEO has revitalized the brand's appeal to younger, digitally native luxury consumers.
Jean-Jacques Guiony serves as President and CEO, with Alexandre Arnault acting as Deputy CEO, leading the division's strategic turnaround.
LVMH is the parent conglomerate; Moët Hennessy is the specific division that manages its 26 wine and spirits brands.
While Clos des Lambrays has 14th-century roots, Ruinart (founded 1729) is the oldest established Champagne House in the collection.
While focused on its existing Maisons, the group has expanded into 'experiential' and cultural sectors in 2025 and 2026 to enhance the lifestyle surrounding its beverage brands."},{"url":"https://www.luxurytribune.com/en/hennessy-embarks-on-a-major-strategic-shift","title":"Hennessy Embarks on a Major Strategic Shift - Luxury Tribune","content":"On June 1, Hennessy unveiled a collection of three ready-to-serve cognac-based cocktails in the United States: Henny-Rita, Henny Berry, and Henny Iced Tea, created by master blender Renaud Fillioux de Gironde.
Behind this launch lies a major strategic shift.
For the first time in over 250 years, the Cognac house is marketing a ready-to-drink product under its own brand."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"LVMH Hennessy new product category launch July 2026 wines and spirits division","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.lvmh.com/en/news-lvmh/hennessy-opens-a-new-chapter-with-its-first-ready-to-serve-cocktails","title":"Hennessy opens a new chapter with its first ready-... - LVMH","content":"# Hennessy opens a new chapter with its first ready-to-serve cocktails​. More than 260 years after its founding, Hennessy is opening a new chapter in its history with the launch of Hennessy Very Special Cocktails, a collection of ready-to-serve cocktails crafted from Hennessy Very Special and the first Hennessy creation to combine cognac with other ingredients. Introduced exclusively in the United States starting July, the range marks a significant milestone for the Maison, bringing its iconic cognac into a new category while remaining true to the expertise and craftsmanship that have defined Hennessy for generations.​. With Hennessy Very Special Cocktails, the Maison continues to explore new ways of enjoying cognac, introducing it to new audiences and new occasions. By bringing together its heritage of craftsmanship with evolving consumer habits, Hennessy reaffirms the pioneering spirit that has shaped its development since its founding and continues to guide its vision for the future.","score":0.7156829,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://wwd.com/business-news/human-resources/lvmh-wines-spirits-hennessy-ceo-shakeup-1236721554","title":"LVMH Shakes Up Wines and Spirits Division With New Appointments","content":"# LVMH Shakes Up Wines and Spirits Division. Jean-Jacques Guiony, previously chief financial officer, will helm the business unit with Alexandre Arnault deployed as deputy chief executive officer. It’s all change at the wines and spirits division of LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton. Jean-Jacques Guiony, the French luxury giant’s longtime chief financial officer, is to become president and chief executive officer of the division, known as Moët Hennessy, with Alexandre Arnault joining him as deputy CEO. Since 2021, Arnault has been executive vice president of product, communication and industrial at Tiffany & Co. in New York, ramping up the American jeweler’s profile with attention-getting campaigns, buzzy ambassadors and new product ranges like its hit Lock line. Bernard Arnault, chairman and CEO of LVMH, revealed the shakeup early Thursday, also appointing Charles Delapalme, previously managing director at Christian Dior Couture, as president and CEO of Hennessy. A 19-year veteran of the group who has worked in senior retail and wholesale executive roles at Louis Vuitton and Fendi, his start date was not specified.","score":0.6793707,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.thespiritsbusiness.com/2026/04/lvmh-starts-2026-with-spirits-uplift","title":"LVMH starts 2026 with spirits uplift - The Spirits Business","content":"The spirits arm of LVMH rose by 5% during the first quarter (Q1) of 2026 with Chinese New Year boosting sales. LVMH’s spirits revenue totalled €610 million (US$719.3m) for Q1, which was down from €629m (US$741.6m) year on year. The division’s 5% organic growth for the first three months of 2026, however, was a contrast to last year’s performance from the same period where LVMH’s spirits arm plunged by 17% due to continued soft demand for Cognac in the US and China. The wine and spirits division was down by 2% a reported basis due to currency fluctuations, which reduced revenue by 7%. For its 2025 full year, LVMH registered a 5% drop for wine and spirits. The Paris-headquartered firm highlighted ‘favourable Chinese New Year phasing’ for Hennessy Cognac in the improved showing for the first quarter of the year.
